verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And one of them, Caiaphas, being the High Priest that same year, said unto them, Ye know nothing at all, nor consider that it is expedient that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not,” &c.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The heathen are stuck fast in the destruction which they made; in the trap which they hid is their foot taken.” ( Ps. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This hath been the case with the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They said that they would kill Jesus, lest the Romans should come and take away their place and nation; and when they had killed Him, these things happened unto them, and when they had done that by doing which they thought to escape, they yet did not escape.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He who was slain is in Heaven, and they who slew have for their portion hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet they did not consider these things; but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They desired,” It saith, “from that day forth to kill Him” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 53 ), for they said, “The Romans will come, and will take away our nation; and a certain one of them, Caiaphas, being High Priest that year, said,” (being more shameless than the rest,) “Ye know nothing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What the others made matter of doubt, and put forth in the way of deliberation, this man cried aloud, shamelessly, openly, audaciously.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye know nothing, nor consider that it is expedient that one man should die, and that the whole nation perish not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And this spake he not of himself, but being High Priest he prophesied.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how great is the force of the High Priest’s authority? or, since he had in any wise been deemed worthy of the High Priesthood, although unworthy thereof, he prophesied, not knowing what he said; and the grace merely made use of his mouth, but touched not his accursed heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed many others have foretold things to come, although unworthy to do so, as Nebuchadnezzar, Pharaoh, Balaam; and the reason of all is evident.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what he saith is of this kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye still sit quiet, ye give heed but carelessly to this matter, and know not how to despise one man’s safety for the sake of the community.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See how great is the power of the Spirit; from an evil imagination It was able to bring forth words full of marvelous prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist calleth the Gentiles “children of God,” from what was about to be: as also Christ Himself saith, “Other sheep I have” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 ), so calling them from what should afterwards come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is, “being High Priest that year”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This matter as well as the rest had become corrupt; for from the time that offices became matters of purchase, they were no longer priests for the whole period of their lives, but for a year.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Notwithstanding, even in this state of things the Spirit was still present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when they lifted up their hands against Christ, then It left them, and removed to the Apostles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This the rending of the veil declared, and the voice of Christ which said, “Behold, your house is left unto you desolate.” ( Matt. xxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Josephus, who lived a short time after, saith, that certain Angels who yet remained with them, (to see) if they would alter their ways, left them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: While the vineyard stood, all things went on; but when they had slain the Heir, no longer so, but they perished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And God having taken it from the Jews, as a glorious garment from an unprofitable son, gave it to right-minded servants of the Gentiles, leaving the others desolate and naked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was, moreover, no small thing that even an enemy should prophesy this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This might draw over others also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in respect of his will, matters fell out contrariwise, since, when He died, the faithful were on this account delivered from the punishment to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What meaneth, “That He might gather together those near and those afar off” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He made them one Body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The dweller in Rome deemeth the Indians a member of himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is equal to this “gathering together”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Head of all is Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “From that day forth the Jews took counsel to put Him to death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, in truth, had sought to do so before; for the Evangelist saith, “Therefore the Jews sought to kill Him” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 ); and, “Why seek ye to kill Me?” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But then they only sought, now they ratified their determination, and treated the action as their business.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But Jesus walked no more openly in Jewry.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He saveth Himself in a human manner, and this He doth continually.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But I have mentioned the reason for which He often departed and withdrew.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And at this time He dwelt in Ephratah, near the wilderness, and there He tarried with His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How thinkest thou that those disciples were confounded when they beheld Him saving Himself after the manner of a man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After this no man followed Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since the Feast was nigh, all were running to Jerusalem; but they, at a time when all others were rejoicing and holding solemn assembly, hide themselves, and are in danger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet still they tarried with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they hid themselves in Galilee, at the time of the Passover and the Feast of Tabernacles; and after this again during the Feast, they only of all were with their Master in flight and concealment, manifesting their good will to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hence Luke recordeth that He said, “I abode with you in temptations”; and this He said, showing that they were strengthened by His influence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And many went up from the country to purify themselves.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And the High Priests and Pharisees had commanded that they should lay hands on Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A marvelous purification, with a murderous will, with homicidal intentions, and bloodstained hands!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And they said, Think ye that he will not come to the feast?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By means of the Passover they plotted against Him, and made the time of feasting a time of murder, that is, He there would fall into their hands, because the season summoned Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What impiety!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they needed greater carefulness, and to forgive those who had been taken for the worst offenses, then they attempted to ensnare One who had done no wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet by acting thus they had already not only profited nothing, but become ridiculous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this end coming among them continually He escapeth, and restraineth them when they take counsel to kill Him, and maketh them to be in perplexity, desiring to prick them by the display of His power; that when they took Him, they might know that what had been done was done, not by their power, but by His permission.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For not even at that time could they take Him, and this though Bethany was near; and when they did take Him, He cast them backwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ch. xii. ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then six days before the Passover He came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, and feasted with them; and Martha served, but Lazarus sat at meat.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This was a proof of the genuineness of his resurrection, that after many days he both lived and ate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And Martha ministered”; whence it is clear that the meal was in her house, for they received Jesus as loving and beloved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some, however, say, that it took place in the house of another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Mary did not minister, for she was a disciple.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again she acted in the more spiritual manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For she did not minister as being invited, nor did she afford her services to all alike.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But she directeth the honor to Him alone, and approacheth Him not as a man, but as a God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account she poured out the ointment, and wiped (His feet) with the hairs of her head, which was the action of one who did not entertain the same opinion concerning Him as did others; yet Judas rebuked her, under the pretense forsooth of carefulness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “She hath done a good work for My burying.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why did He not expose the disciple in the case of the woman, nor say to him what the Evangelist hath declared, that on account of his own thieving he rebuked her?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In His abundant longsuffering He wished to bring him to a better mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For because He knew that he was a traitor, He from the beginning often rebuked him, saying, “Not all believe,” and, “One of you is a devil.” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showed them that He knew him to be a traitor, yet He did not openly rebuke him, but bare with him, desiring to recall him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith another Evangelist, that all the disciples used these words? ( Matt. xxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All used them, and so did he, but the others not with like purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if any one ask why He put the bag of the poor in the hands of a thief, and made him steward who was a lover of money, we would reply, that God knoweth the secret reason; but that, if we may say something by conjecture, it was that He might cut off from him all excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he could not say that he did this thing from love of money, (for he had in the bag sufficient to allay his desire,) but from excessive wickedness which Christ wished to restrain, using much condescension towards him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He did not even rebuke him as stealing, although aware of it, stopping the way to his wicked desire, and taking from him all excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Let her alone,” He saith, “for against the day of My burying hath she done this.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, He maketh mention of the traitor in speaking of His burial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But him the reproof reacheth not, nor doth the expression soften him, though sufficient to inspire him with pity: as if He had said, “I am burdensome and troublesome, but wait a little while, and I shall depart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This too he intended in saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But Me ye have not always.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But none of these things turned back that savage madman; yet in truth Jesus said and did far more than this, He washed his feet that night, made him a sharer in the table and the salt, a thing which is wont to restrain even the souls of robbers, and spake other words, enough to melt a stone, and this, not long before, but on the very day, in order that not even time might cause it to be forgotten.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he stood out against all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a dreadful, a dreadful thing is the love of money, it disables both eyes and ears, and makes men worse to deal with than a wild beast, allowing a man to consider neither conscience, nor friendship, nor fellowship, nor the salvation of his own soul, but having withdrawn them at once from all these things, like some harsh mistress, it makes those captured by it its slaves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the dreadful part of so bitter a slavery is, that it persuades them even to be grateful for it; and the more they become enslaved, the more doth their pleasure increase; and in this way especially the malady becomes incurable, in this way the monster becomes hard to conquer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This made Gehazi a leper instead of a disciple and a prophet; this destroyed Ananias and her with him; this made Judas a traitor; this corrupted the rulers of the Jews, who received gifts, and became the partners of thieves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This hath brought in ten thousand wars, filling the ways with blood, the cities with wailings and lamentations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This hath made meals to become impure, and tables accursed, and hath filled food with transgression; therefore hath Paul called it “idolatry”: ( Col. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ), and not even so hath he deterred men from it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why calleth he it “idolatry”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many possess wealth, and dare not use it, but consecrate it, handing it down untouched, not daring to touch it, as though it were some dedicated thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if at any time they are forced to do so, they feel as though they had done something unlawful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, as the Greek carefully tends his graven image, so thou entrusteth thy gold to doors and bars; providing a chest instead of a shrine, and laying it up in silver vessels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But thou dost not bow down to it as he to the image?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet thou showest all kind of attention to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, he would rather give up his eyes or his life than his graven image.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So also would those who love gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith one, “I worship not the gold.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Neither doth he, he saith, worship the image, but the devil that dwelleth in it; and in like manner thou, though thou worship not the gold, yet thou worshipest that devil who springeth on thy soul, from the sight of the gold and thy lust for it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For more grievous than an evil spirit is the lust of money-loving, and many obey it more than others do idols.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these last in many things disobey, but in this case they yield everything, and whatever it telleth them to do, they obey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What saith it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Be at war with all,” it saith, “at enmity with all, know not nature, despise God, sacrifice to me thyself,” and in all they obey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To the graven images they sacrifice oxen and sheep, but avarice saith, Sacrifice to me thine own soul, and the man obeyeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou what kind of altars it hath, what kind of sacrifices it receiveth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The covetous shall not inherit the Kingdom of God, but not even so do they fear. ( 1 Cor. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet this desire is weaker than all the others, it is not inborn, nor natural, (for then it would have been placed in us at the beginning;) but there was no gold at the beginning, and no man desired gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if you will, I will tell you whence the mischief entered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By each man’s envying the one before him, men have increased the disease, and he who has gotten in advance provokes him who had no desire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when men see splendid houses, and extensive lands, and troops of slaves, and silver vessels, and great heaps of apparel, they use every means to outdo them; so that the first set of men are causes of the second, and these of those who come after.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if they would be sober-minded, they would not be teachers (of evil) to others; yet neither have these any excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For others there are also who despise riches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And who,” saith one, “despises them?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the terrible thing is, that, because wickedness is so general, this seems to have become impossible, and it is not even believed that one can act aright.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shall I then mention many both in cities and in the mountains?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what would it avail?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye will not from their example become better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, our discourse hath not now this purpose, that you should empty yourselves of your substance: I would that ye could do so; however, since the burden is too heavy for you, I constrain you not; only I advise you that you desire not what belongs to others, that you impart somewhat of your own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many such we shall find, contented with what belongs to them, taking care of their own, and living on honest labor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why do we not rival and imitate these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us think of those who have gone before us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do not their possessions stand, preserving nothing but their name; such an one’s bath, such an one’s suburban seat and lodging?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do we not, when we behold them, straightway groan, when we consider what toil he endured, what rapine committed? and now he is nowhere seen, but others luxuriate in his possessions, men whom he never expected would do so, perhaps even his enemies, while he is suffering extremest punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things await us also; for we shall certainly die, and shall certainly have to submit to the same end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How much wrath, tell me, how much expense, how many enmities these men incurred; and what the gain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Deathless punishment, and the having no consolation; and the being not only while alive, but when gone, accused by all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What? when we see the images of the many laid up in their houses, shall we not weep the more?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of a truth well said the Prophet, “Verily, every man living disquieteth himself in vain” ( Ps. xxxix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 11 , LXX.); for anxiety about such things is indeed disquiet, disquiet and superfluous trouble.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But it is not so in the everlasting mansions, not so in those tabernacles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here one hath labored, and another enjoys; but there each shall possess his own labors, and shall receive a manifold reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us press forward to get that possession, there let us prepare for ourselves houses, that we may rest in Christ Jesus our Lord, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, for ever and ever.
